reason.Under 50 hours: Under 50 documented hours falls short of the 200-hour USA J-1 minimum, so build and log more childcare time first.
reason.50–200 hours: Between 50 and 200 hours is close but below the J-1 threshold, so add documented hours before applying.
reason.200–500 hours: Over 200 documented hours meets the USA J-1 minimum and satisfies most European programmes.
reason.500+ hours: More than 500 documented hours is a strong childcare record that host families value highly.
reason.Not sure: Unsure of your documented hours, so total and verify them since sponsors require proof of at least 200.

reason.3+ contactable references: Three or more contactable references meet the typical J-1 sponsor requirement.
reason.1–2 references: One or two references is a start, but most sponsors want three non-family written references.
reason.None yet: No childcare references yet is a blocker, so line up three contactable referees now.

reason.Under 12 months (infant): Infant experience opens the under-2 placements many host families specifically request.
reason.1–2 years (toddler): Toddler experience is widely sought and covers most host-family needs.
reason.3–5 years (preschool): Preschool-age experience suits a broad range of placements.
reason.6–12 years (school age): School-age experience is useful but limits the infant and toddler placements families often prioritise.
reason.13+ years (teen): Only teen experience narrows your options, since most au-pair roles centre on younger children.
reason.Not sure: Unclear youngest age cared for, so confirm it as infant experience widens your placement options.

reason.Current (within 2 years): A current first aid or CPR certificate reassures host families and meets common programme expectations.
reason.Expired: Your first aid certificate has expired, so renew it before applying.
reason.None: No first aid or CPR certificate yet, so book a course since many sponsors and families expect one.

ranges.reason: Under 18 is below every au-pair programme age floor, so you cannot apply yet.
Aged 18 to 26 sits inside the USA J-1 and most European age windows, the ideal band.
Aged 27 to 30 rules out the USA J-1 but France still accepts you up to 30.
Over 30 is above the age cap for the main au-pair programmes, so the standard routes are closed.

reason.Code B (full): A full Code B licence meets the USA J-1 driving requirement and most European host-family expectations.
reason.Learners only: A learners licence is not enough, so finish your full Code B before applying since J-1 effectively requires it.
reason.In progress: Your licence is still in progress, so complete the full Code B as J-1 placements depend on it.
reason.None: No driver's licence is a major blocker for J-1 and most European families, so start the process now.

ranges.reason: No children of your own keeps you eligible for the standard au-pair programmes.
Having children of your own conflicts with most au-pair programme rules, so seek specialist guidance on the few that allow it.

reason.Valid — 2+ years remaining: Your passport has enough validity for a 12-month placement and visa.
reason.Valid — under 2 years remaining: Your passport is valid but renew it soon, as visas usually need validity beyond the placement.
reason.Expired: An expired passport must be renewed before any application can move forward.
reason.No passport: No passport is the first blocker to clear, so apply immediately.

reason.Current (within 6 months): A current SAPS clearance keeps your application visa-ready.
reason.Older than 6 months: Your police clearance is over six months old, so a fresh SAPS certificate will likely be required.
reason.Applied / in progress: Your SAPS clearance is in progress, which is on track since turnaround runs several weeks.
reason.None: No police clearance yet, so apply now as SAPS turnaround is typically four to eight weeks.

reason.Under R15k: Under R15k is below the typical R15k to R30k pre-departure budget for fees, apostille, visa and medical.
reason.R15k – R30k: R15k to R30k covers the usual au-pair pre-departure costs.
reason.R30k – R60k: R30k to R60k gives a comfortable buffer for pre-departure costs and a flight.
reason.R60k+: Over R60k is a strong financial position for the move.
reason.Prefer not to say: Budget not declared, so share it privately if it shapes your timing since pre-departure costs run R15k to R30k.

reason.Native / first language: English as a first language clears every host-family interview comfortably.
reason.Fluent: Fluent English is more than enough for host-family interviews and daily childcare.
reason.Conversational: Conversational English handles most interviews, though stronger fluency widens your placement options.
reason.Basic: Basic English is a real blocker, since host families interview and place in English.

reason.Matric (NSC): A completed matric meets the secondary-education requirement for the USA J-1 programme.
reason.Diploma / Certificate: A post-school diploma exceeds the secondary-education bar and strengthens your profile.
reason.Bachelor degree: A degree exceeds the education requirement and appeals to host families.
reason.Honours / Postgrad: A postgraduate qualification comfortably exceeds the education requirement.
reason.Currently studying: Still studying may meet the bar, so confirm a completed matric since J-1 requires secondary education.
reason.Other: A non-standard qualification may need checking against the secondary-education requirement for J-1.

reason.As soon as possible: An ASAP timeline lets you start matching with host families now.
reason.Within 6 months: A six-month horizon is realistic once your documents and references are ready.
reason.Within 1 year: A one-year horizon gives ample room for references, the licence and visa paperwork.
reason.Just exploring: Just exploring means no urgency, so you can plan the documents and hours at your own pace.

reason.6 months: A six-month commitment suits some European placements but rules out the 12-month J-1 minimum.
reason.12 months: A 12-month commitment matches the J-1 minimum and most European programmes.
reason.12 months + extension: Willingness to extend beyond 12 months is attractive to host families and fits the J-1 extension.
reason.24 months (USA max): Committing up to the 24-month J-1 maximum is highly appealing to host families.
reason.Not sure: Undecided on contract length, so note that the J-1 needs a 12-month minimum when you plan.
